description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its length. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a uniform dark coloration, while the top surface displays a lighter, marbled stone texture. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a solid stone material such as granite or marble. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to prevent vehicle passage or to delineate pedestrian zones. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional components.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides. The top features alternating raised and recessed stone bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flat face with horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves, which now run vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooved pattern across the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
